title: Apply: The Original Constitution
description: Apply your understanding of the articles and sections of the Constitution in this set of free practice questions.
topic_path: Civil Dialogue, The Constitution, and Constitutional Interpretation

assessment_items.question_content:
The president, with their staff, negotiates a peace treaty with another country. Nearly all (94 out of a possible 100) of the Senators present vote to approve the treaty.

**Which part of the Constitution describes this process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Article I
Article II
Article III
Article IV

assessment_items.question_content:
Due to an increasing number of federal cases, the Judicial Conference recommends to Congress that it creates a new district and court of appeals judgeships.

**Which part of the Constitution grants Congress this power?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Article I
Article II
Article III
Article IV

assessment_items.question_content:
Many people want to change Puerto Rico's status as a U.S. territory and add it as the 51st state. 

**Where in the Constitution could someone find the process for adding a new state?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Article I
Article II
Article III
Article IV

assessment_items.question_content:
Amanda wants to propose an amendment to the Constitution. 

**Where could she find the amendment process described in the Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Article IV
Article V
Article VI
Article VII

assessment_items.question_content:
Maryland approves an over-the-counter medication that treats the common cold. However, the Federal Drug Administration has prohibited the sale of that medication because it has not been approved by their department. Pharmacies in Maryland then have to stop selling the medication.

**What part of the Constitution gives the federal government the power to prohibit  the sale of the drug?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supremacy Clause
The Necessary and Proper Clause
The Elections Clause
The Full Faith and Credit Clause

assessment_items.question_content:
Jaime lives in Alabama and is on vacation in Florida, where he is accused of a crime. As he’s arrested, Jaime is told by the police officer that if he cannot afford an attorney, the state of Florida will provide one for him. 

**Which part of the Constitution guarantees that Jaime receives the same civil rights in Florida as he would in Alabama?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supremacy Clause
The Necessary and Proper Clause
The Privileges or Immunities Clause
The Citizenship Clause

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Article VI of the U.S. Constitution.**

This Constitution, and the Laws of the United States which shall be made in Pursuance thereof; and all Treaties made, or which shall be made, under the Authority of the United States, shall be the supreme Law of the Land; and the Judges in every State shall be bound thereby, any Thing in the Constitution or Laws of any State to the Contrary notwithstanding.

**What is the purpose of this section of the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To create the requirements that the Constitution must meet to be ratified
To establish that the Constitution is legal and takes precedence over state laws
To frame the structure of the three branches of the federal government
To describe the types of cases that the Supreme Court must hear

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt of Article VII of the United States Constitution.**

The Ratification of the Conventions of nine States, shall be sufficient for the Establishment of this Constitution between the States so ratifying the Same.

**Which statement best describes the significance of Article VII?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It establishes that only some of the states were needed to ratify the Constitution.
It reserves specific rights for state governments that the federal government does not have.
It explains that states that ratified the Constitution can submit amendments in the future.
It decrees that the U.S. Constitution is the supreme law of the land.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Article I, Section 1 of the U.S. Constitution.**

All legislative Powers herein granted shall be vested in a Congress of the United States, which shall consist of a Senate and House of Representatives.

**What is the purpose of this section of the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To describe the lawmaking process of the federal government
To explain the three branches of the federal government
To establish the lawmaking branch of government
To disband and replace existing state governments

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Article IV, Section 2 of the U.S. Constitution.**

The Citizens of each State shall be entitled to all Privileges and Immunities of Citizens in the several States.

**What is the purpose of this section of the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To list the rights and freedoms that the federal government cannot interfere with
To establish the concept of birthright citizenship for anyone born in a U.S. state or territory
To detail the requirements for the naturalization process for immigrants to the United States
To guarantee that the rights of citizens extend beyond the borders of their home states and apply in other states

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Article V of the U.S. Constitution.**

The Congress, whenever two thirds of both Houses shall [[☃ definition 1]] it necessary, shall propose Amendments to this Constitution, or, on the Application of the Legislatures of two thirds of the several States, shall call a Convention for proposing Amendments, which, in either Case, shall be valid to all Intents and Purposes, as Part of this Constitution, when ratified by the Legislatures of three fourths of the several States, or by Conventions in three fourths thereof, as the one or the other Mode of Ratification may be proposed by the Congress.

**What is the purpose of this section of the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It describes how a bill becomes a law.
It describes the election process for the legislative branch.
It describes the specific powers of the legislative branch.
It describes how to change the Constitution.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from Article III, Section 2 of the U.S. Constitution.**

In all Cases affecting Ambassadors, other public Ministers and Consuls, and those in which a State shall be Party, the supreme Court shall have original Jurisdiction. In all the other Cases before mentioned, the supreme Court shall have [[☃ definition 1]] Jurisdiction, both as to Law and Fact, with such Exceptions, and under such Regulations as the Congress shall make.

**What is the purpose of this section of the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: related to a court that hears appeals of lower courts’ decisions

choices.content: It describes the different government officials who must be appointed by the executive branch.
It describes the nomination and appointment process for justices who serve on the Supreme Court.
It describes which types of cases only the Supreme Court has the authority to hear and decide.
It describes the foreign policy powers of the legislative and executive branches of the federal government.
